THOUGHT: The miscompilation arises because InstCombine incorrectly propagates the 'ninf' fast-math flag to the synthesized fabs() when folding the select of two comparisons. In the source, only one branch (%cmp1) has 'ninf' and is conditionally selected by %cmp2; applying 'ninf' to the fabs() makes it poison on -inf even when the select would have chosen false, leading to increased poison. The fix is to avoid unioning FMF from separate conditions; instead, intersect or drop unsafe flags.
